source_papers.reference: SkillRL: Skill-Based Reinforcement Learning for LLM Agents with Automatic Skill Discovery
source_papers.justification: Core framework for automatic skill abstraction and hierarchical skill library construction from agent experiences via RL
source_papers.usage: Key methodological reference for skill discovery, SKILLBANK organization, and recursive skill evolution

source_papers.reference: ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs
source_papers.justification: Established framework for LLM tool-use with large-scale API integration, neural API retriever (ToolRetriever), and DFSDT reasoning
source_papers.usage: Reference for tool/skill retrieval architecture and standardized tool API format

source_papers.reference: AgentSkillOS: Leveraging, Managing, and Scaling the Agent Skill Ecosystem
source_papers.justification: OS for discovering, composing, and running skill pipelines from 90K+ skills using skill tree construction, LLM-guided retrieval, and DAG orchestration
source_papers.usage: Reference for skill organization, retrieval, and pipeline composition architecture

source_papers.reference: SkillsBench: Evaluating How Agent Skills Improve AI Agent Performance
source_papers.justification: First benchmark for evaluating agent skill effectiveness across 11 domains (86 tasks, 7300+ trials). Shows +16pp average, +51.9pp in healthcare
source_papers.usage: Evidence that skills are especially beneficial in healthcare; evaluation methodology reference

source_papers.reference: TxAgent: An AI Agent for Therapeutic Reasoning Across a Universe of Tools
source_papers.justification: Medical agent with ToolUniverse (211 biomedical tools), ToolGen (multi-agent tool construction from API docs), and ToolRAG (adaptive tool retrieval). Achieves 92.1% accuracy on drug reasoning
source_papers.usage: Key reference for automated medical tool construction (ToolGen) and domain-specific tool universe design

source_papers.reference: MCP-Zero: Active Tool Discovery for Autonomous LLM Agents
source_papers.justification: Active agent framework enabling LLMs to autonomously discover tools on-demand via MCP. Uses hierarchical semantic routing across 2797 tools with 98% token reduction
source_papers.usage: Reference for MCP-based active tool discovery and iterative capability extension

source_papers.reference: HuggingGPT: Solving AI Tasks with ChatGPT and its Friends in Hugging Face
source_papers.justification: Foundational work on LLM-as-controller connecting to expert models via API. Four-stage pipeline: task planning, model selection, task execution, response generation
source_papers.usage: Foundational reference for wrapping ML models as callable tools for an LLM controller

source_papers.reference: MedAgentGym: Training LLM Agents for Code-Based Medical Reasoning at Scale
source_papers.justification: ICLR 2026 paper on training medical LLM agents with code-based tool use. Demonstrates SFT and DPO for medical agent training
source_papers.usage: Reference for medical agent training methodology and code-based medical reasoning

source_papers.reference: MedSAM-Agent: Empowering Interactive Medical Image Segmentation with Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
source_papers.justification: Direct intersection of medical segmentation and agentic RL. Multi-turn interaction with MedSAM via reinforcement learning for iterative refinement
source_papers.usage: Template for how Medical Agent invokes segmentation skills with iterative agentic refinement

task2:
Build a universal Medical Model-to-Skill wrapping pipeline and an LLM-powered Medical Agent that can discover, select, and compose Medical Skills for complex clinical workflows, targeting MICCAI 2026.

Core Idea: Design a generic wrapping framework that can automatically convert any medical AI model (segmentation, classification, detection, registration, report generation, VQA, etc.) into a standardized Medical Skill using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) as the interoperability layer. The pipeline takes a medical model (with its code, weights, and API documentation) as input and produces a self-describing MCP-compatible Medical Skill as output — complete with structured input/output schemas, medical metadata (modality, anatomy, task type), safety constraints, and validation checks.

Key Components:
(1) Med-SkillGen: A universal wrapping pipeline (inspired by TxAgent's ToolGen) that auto-generates MCP-compatible Medical Skills from existing medical AI models. This includes: (a) automatic API analysis and schema extraction from model code/documentation, (b) medical metadata annotation (imaging modality, anatomical region, clinical task type, safety level), (c) standardized I/O interface generation following MCP tool specification, and (d) validation test generation to ensure skill correctness.
(2) Med-SkillLib: A curated Medical Skills library built by applying Med-SkillGen to existing open-source medical models (MedSAM, MedSAM2, CheXagent, etc.) and existing Medical MCP servers (DICOM-MCP, healthcare-mcp-public, medical-calculator-MCP, etc.). The library organizes skills hierarchically by modality, anatomy, and task type.
(3) Med-Agent: An LLM-powered agent that uses active skill discovery (inspired by MCP-Zero) to dynamically retrieve and compose Medical Skills from Med-SkillLib for multi-step clinical workflows. The agent performs task planning, skill selection via hierarchical semantic routing, execution graph construction, and result integration.

The key innovation bridges two worlds: (a) general-purpose agent skill frameworks (ToolLLM, AgentSkillOS, SkillRL, MCP-Zero) that lack medical domain expertise, and (b) isolated medical AI tools that lack interoperability and composability. By using MCP as the universal protocol, any medical model can be 'plugged in' as a skill, enabling the agent to solve complex clinical scenarios (e.g., 'Given this chest CT, segment the lungs, detect nodules, measure their size, assess malignancy risk, and generate a structured report') through dynamic skill composition.

This aligns with MICCAI 2026's theme 'From Algorithms to Clinical Translation' by making individual medical AI algorithms composable and deployable through a unified agent framework.
